canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, NewsQA is consistently described as a measurement instrument used to evaluate question answering and machine comprehension. The dataset is composed of news articles or "news-related passages," which are characterized as being long and realistic. According to the definitions, successful performance on NewsQA requires models to engage in both "comprehension and extraction of answers" and "synthesizing information" from these passages. One paper notes the use of an "MRQA version" of the dataset for testing purposes.
papers: AGD: Adversarial Game Defense Against Jailbreak Attacks in Large Language Models
Collapse of Dense Retrievers: Short, Early, and Literal Biases Outranking Factual Evidence
PrivaCI-Bench: Evaluating Privacy with Contextual Integrity and Legal Compliance
AdaptThink: Reasoning Models Can Learn When to Think
so much depends / upon / a whitespace: Why Whitespace Matters for Poets andLLMs

definitions.text: Question answering dataset based on news articles, requiring comprehension and extraction of answers from long, realistic passages.
definitions.paper: Collapse of Dense Retrievers: Short, Early, and Literal Biases Outranking Factual Evidence
